economic_notes: Construction in progress includes bridge replacement project ($4,022,712), water treatment plant ($402,600), and two CMHC Section 95 housing projects. CMHC advanced $1,135,397 of $1,580,178 total mortgage proceeds for two Section 95 projects not yet completed. COVID-19 noted as subsequent event.
notes: Fiscal year ended March 31, 2020. Audit report dated April 1, 2022 — over two years after year-end, which is highly unusual. Five consolidated entities (Government, Education Authority, Health Program, Employment and Training, Housing Authority). No GBEs reported. Net debt position of $4,996,412. CMHC advance payable of $1,135,397 for incomplete Section 95 housing projects. Two legal claims outstanding: $228,520 (Hanslip Excavating) and $140,488 (Sundance Construction). COVID-19 noted as subsequent event. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $7,512,434 + CMHC $172,006 + ESDC $241,801 + CMHC accounts receivable offset = $7,926,241 per revenue lines; OSR includes rental income, other revenue net of recoveries and deferred adjustments.
